content: Hypertension is defined as sustained elevated blood pressure. ACC/AHA 2017 guidelines define Stage 1 as SBP 130-139 or DBP 80-89 mmHg; Stage 2 as SBP ≥140 or DBP ≥90 mmHg. JNC8 and European guidelines use ≥140/90 threshold. Diagnosis requires average of ≥2 readings on ≥2 occasions. White coat hypertension (elevated in office, normal at home) and masked hypertension (normal in office, elevated at home) require ambulatory or home monitoring for accurate diagnosis. Primary (essential) hypertension accounts for 90-95% of cases; secondary causes include renal disease, primary aldosteronism, obstructive sleep apnea, thyroid disorders, and medications (NSAIDs, decongestants, oral contraceptives).
